note_data.title: Chemical energetics
keyPoints: Transition elements are found in the d-block of the periodic table and form one or more stable ions with incomplete d orbitals
The d subshell can hold up to 10 electrons
Scandium and zinc are in the d-block but are not transition elements because their ions do not have incomplete d subshells
Scandium forms Sc³⁺ with an empty d subshell [Ar], zinc forms Zn²⁺ with a full d subshell [Ar]3d¹⁰
The 4s orbital is filled before the 3d orbital, but 4s electrons are removed before 3d electrons when forming ions
Transition elements commonly form multiple oxidation states due to similar energy levels of 3d and 4s electrons
Chromium has an unusual electron configuration [Ar]3d⁵4s¹ rather than the expected [Ar]3d⁴4s²
Copper also has an unusual electron configuration [Ar]3d¹⁰4s¹ rather than the expected [Ar]3d⁹4s²

definitions.definition: Elements that form one or more stable ions with incomplete d orbitals
Atomic orbitals that can hold up to 10 electrons and have a characteristic shape

howToAnswer.instruction: Give the definition or fact in one sentence with no explanation
Show all working clearly. Include units in final answer. Use appropriate number of significant figures
Give reason using 'because' or 'this is because'. Link cause and effect clearly
For energy level diagrams, label axes, reactants, products, and ΔH with arrow

modelAnswer.question: Calculate the enthalpy of formation of methane using the following combustion data: ΔHc°[C] = -394 kJ mol⁻¹, ΔHc°[H₂] = -286 kJ mol⁻¹, ΔHc°[CH₄] = -890 kJ mol⁻¹
modelAnswer.answer:
C(s) + 2H₂(g) → CH₄(g)
Using Hess's Law:
ΔHf°[CH₄] = ΔHc°[C] + 2ΔHc°[H₂] - ΔHc°[CH₄]
ΔHf°[CH₄] = (-394) + 2(-286) - (-890)
ΔHf°[CH₄] = -394 - 572 + 890 = -76 kJ mol⁻¹
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correct equation for formation of methane
Mark 4: Correct final answer with units

commonMistakes: Thinking scandium and zinc are transition elements - they are d-block elements but not transition elements
Removing 3d electrons before 4s electrons when forming ions - 4s electrons are always removed first
Forgetting that chromium and copper have unusual electron configurations with only one 4s electron
examinerTips: Remember the definition requires incomplete d orbitals in the ion, not just being in the d-block
Always remove 4s electrons first when writing ion electron configurations, even though 4s fills before 3d
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Energy level diagram showing reactants and products connected by arrow labeled ΔH, with energy on y-axis and reaction pathway on x-axis. Activation energy shown as peak between reactants and products.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q5 (6 marks) — they asked students to calculate the energy change for combustion of methanol using bond energies and explain whether the reaction was exothermic or endothermic
Featured in O/N 2022 P1 Q18 (1 mark) — multiple choice question asking students to identify which energy profile diagram correctly showed an exothermic reaction with activation energy labeled
Appeared in M/J 2022 P2 Q4(c) (4 marks) — students had to draw and label an energy profile diagram for an endothermic reaction, showing reactants, products, activation energy and enthalpy change
Tested in O/N 2023 P2 Q6 (5 marks) — calculation question requiring students to use average bond energies to determine enthalpy change for the formation of ammonia from nitrogen and hydrogen

workedExample.question: Calculate the enthalpy change for the combustion of ethane using the average bond energies given. C₂H₆ + 3½O₂ → 2CO₂ + 3H₂O. Bond energies (kJ/mol): C-C = 347, C-H = 413, O=O = 498, C=O = 805, O-H = 464 (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify bonds broken in reactants: C₂H₆ has 1 C-C bond and 6 C-H bonds; 3½O₂ has 3.5 O=O bonds
Step 2: Calculate energy required to break bonds = (1 × 347) + (6 × 413) + (3.5 × 498) = 347 + 2478 + 1743 = 4568 kJ/mol
Step 3: Identify bonds formed in products: 2CO₂ has 4 C=O bonds; 3H₂O has 6 O-H bonds
Step 4: Calculate energy released when bonds form = (4 × 805) + (6 × 464) = 3220 + 2784 = 6004 kJ/mol
Step 5: Calculate enthalpy change: ΔH = Energy required - Energy released = 4568 - 6004 = -1436 kJ/mol
workedExample.answer: ΔH = -1436 kJ/mol (negative value indicates exothermic reaction)
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for identifying correct bonds broken, 1 mark for correct calculation of energy to break bonds, 1 mark for identifying correct bonds formed, 1 mark for correct calculation of energy released, 1 mark for correct final calculation, 1 mark for correct sign and units

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused the signs for exothermic and endothermic reactions' — always remember that exothermic reactions have NEGATIVE ΔH values and endothermic reactions have POSITIVE ΔH values

Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to include activation energy when drawing energy profile diagrams' — always show and label the activation energy as the energy barrier from reactants to the peak of the curve

Examiner Report 2023: 'Common error was incorrect calculation of bonds broken versus bonds formed' — remember that energy is REQUIRED to break bonds (positive) and energy is RELEASED when bonds form (negative)
note_data.keyDiagram: Energy profile diagram showing: y-axis labeled 'Energy' and x-axis labeled 'Reaction pathway/Progress of reaction'. Draw horizontal line for reactants energy level, higher/lower horizontal line for products. Curved line connecting them with peak showing activation energy (Ea). Label ΔH as vertical arrow between reactant and product levels. For exothermic: products lower than reactants, ΔH arrow points down. For endothermic: products higher than reactants, ΔH arrow points up.
note_data.quickRecap: Transition elements are d-block elements that form stable ions with incomplete d orbitals. Scandium and zinc are excluded as their ions have empty or full d subshells respectively. When forming ions, 4s electrons are removed before 3d electrons despite filling order. Many transition elements form multiple oxidation states due to similar energy levels of 3d and 4s orbitals.
